subject: Math
skill: Fact Fluency and Number Bonds Within 10

lesson.story_mission: Power up Bond Bridge with number pairs to 10.
lesson.mini_lesson: Number bonds show how two parts make a whole.

worked_examples.text: If the whole is 10 and one part is 6, the missing part is 4.

hint_ladder: Count up from the known part.

misconception_bank.bond_missing_part: Learner is not yet decomposing from the whole.
misconception_bank.bond_pair_error: Learner confuses near pairs with exact bonds.
misconception_bank.fact_fluency_slow: Learner needs repeated practice on doubles.

question_audio.text: What number bond makes 5: 0 plus blank equals 5?
question_audio.label: Read Question

hints: Look at the whole and the known part.
Count on from the known part to the whole.
The missing part joins the known part to make the whole.
questions.explanation: 0 and 5 are parts that make the whole 5.
questions.feedback: Nice work. 0 and 5 are parts that make the whole 5.

questions.feedback: Good thinking. The missing part is 4 because 2 + 4 = 6.

story.text: Welcome to Bond Bridge. The bridge lights up when you find number pairs that make 10. Your goal is to use number bonds to see parts and wholes. You will learn how two parts can join to make a whole number and how to find a missing part. Press Start Mission when you are ready.
story.label: Read This Page

lesson.text: This lesson is about fact fluency and number bonds within 10. Important words are part, whole, number bond, plus, equals, and missing number. A number bond model shows the whole at the top and the two parts that make it. A common mistake is naming the given part again instead of finding the missing partner, so check that both parts add to the whole. Press Next to continue.
lesson.label: Read This Page

watch.text: Watch this worked example. The whole is 10 and one part is 6, and the number-bond model shows one missing part. Step one, read the number bond and find the whole. Step two, ask what part goes with 6 to make 10. Step three, count on from 6: 7, 8, 9, 10, which is four steps. The final answer is 4 because 6 plus 4 equals 10. Press Next to continue.
watch.label: Read This Page

demo.text: This demo shows how a number bond connects parts to a whole. Watch the whole split into two parts, then check that the parts join back to the same whole. For example, if the whole is 10 and one part is 7, the missing part is 3 because 7 plus 3 equals 10. Press Next to continue.
demo.label: Read This Page

practice.text: This is practice. Use the number-bond strategy: find the whole, name the known part, and count on to find the missing part. The model shows a bond or equation, such as 10 equals 7 plus missing number. You can press Read Question to hear the exact question again. Choose your answer when you are ready.
practice.label: Read This Page

challenge.text: This is a challenge. It is harder because the missing number might appear in a different place in the equation. Use the part-part-whole model and check that the two parts make the whole. You can press Read Question to hear the exact question, and use the hint if you need help.
challenge.label: Read This Page

checkpoint.text: This is your checkpoint. Show that you understand number bonds within 10 and can find a missing part or total. Use counting on, known pairs to 10, and the part-part-whole model. Press Read Question if you want the exact question read aloud. Answer carefully, then continue.
checkpoint.label: Read This Page

badge.text: Great work powering up Bond Bridge. You practiced number bonds, parts and wholes, and pairs that make 10. These facts help addition and subtraction feel faster. Press Next to see your profile.
badge.label: Read This Page

profile.text: This is your growth profile. It can summarize your attempts, correct answers, and accuracy so you know how your number-bond practice went. Your next recommendation will help you keep building fluency within 10. You can replay the mission or go back to the hub.
profile.label: Read This Page
